title: Rep. Moulton prohibits staff from prediction market betting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rep. Seth Moulton bans staff from using prediction market platforms, citing ethical concerns and insider influence risks.
The policy prohibits trading related to political, legislative, or geopolitical outcomes, effective immediately for Moulton's congressional office.
Moulton emphasizes integrity, urging other officials to maintain high ethical standards and avoid profiting from policy-related information.

title: Livestream: Closing arguments in the Kelsey Fitzsimmons trial
author: John Waller
bullets: Closing arguments in the trial of former North Andover officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons are being livestreamed via NBC10 Boston.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons opted for a bench trial, where a judge will decide the outcome rather than a jury.
The trial features testimony from an officer involved in the shooting of Kelsey Fitzsimmons.

title: Plymouth police officer, husband arrested amid allegations of sexual abuse
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Officer Samantha Pelrine and husband Daniel Forand arrested at home, facing multiple charges including aggravated child rape.
Pelrine placed on leave amid allegations of off-duty sexual abuse; arraignment to occur at Plymouth District Court.
Plymouth Police Department expresses disturbance at allegations, commits to thorough internal investigation.

title: Jayson Tatum explains what’s allowed Jaylen Brown to turn into an MVP contender
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown's standout season sees him leading the Boston Celtics with career-high averages in points, rebounds, and assists.
With Jayson Tatum injured, Brown has embraced being Boston's primary scorer, elevating the team's title aspirations.
Jaylen Brown is considered an MVP contender, bolstered by Celtics' improved roster performance under coach Joe Mazzulla.

title: In a fitting turn, Casey Mittelstadt delivered in one of Bruins’ gutsiest wins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Casey Mittelstadt scored his 14th goal to tie the game, leading to the Bruins' 4-3 overtime win against the Sabres.
The Bruins overcame a previous loss to the Maple Leafs, securing a crucial victory against top NHL team Buffalo.
Boston now has 88 points, positioning three points ahead of Ottawa and the New York Islanders in the playoff race.

title: Why is Jaylen Brown getting more trips to the free-throw line? ‘Maybe complaining’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown's comments on officiating may be influencing more free-throw calls in his favor.
Brown scored 31 points and made 12 of 14 free throws in a Celtics win over the OKC Thunder.
The Celtics had 29 free-throw attempts compared to 26 by the Thunder during the game.

title: How did James Hagens fare in his pro debut with the Providence Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens made his pro debut with Providence Bruins, recording zero points in a 2-1 victory over Springfield.
Hagens showcased his skating ability and landed three shots on net, contributing to P-Bruins' neutral zone play.
Although facing early challenges, the 19-year-old forward showed flashes of skill with effective power play participation.

title: Jaylen Brown leads Celtics to statement win over Thunder: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown scored 31 points to lead the Celtics to a 119-109 victory over the Oklahoma City Thunder.
Brown and teammate Jayson Tatum combined for 15 assists, helping the Celtics to overcome OKC's defense.
Baylor Scheierman scored 11 points, contributing as a bench player since Tatum's return to the lineup.

title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Anthony Shearrion and Arkie Swinson of Boston face assault charges following a parking dispute, according to court records.
The incident occurred in Dorchester, where the victim hit their car while parking, leading to an alleged assault.
Both suspects pleaded not guilty and were held at Suffolk County Jail after failing to post bail; court date set for April 14.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox are preparing for the 2026 season with questions about lineup efficiency, bullpen performance, and infield defense.
Boston's starting rotation is bolstered by additions like Sonny Gray and Ranger Suarez, aiming to support their run-prevention strategy.
Outfield depth creates a challenge in managing playing time, with Jarren Duran currently set as the designated hitter.
